name: Community Roles | openEuler
description: Learn about the different roles within the openEuler community - contributor, committer, and maintainer.

mainEntity.name: What are the roles in openEuler SIG groups?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main roles in openEuler SIG groups include: Maintainer (responsible for final code review and merging), Committer (has merge permissions for specific scopes), and Contributor (participates through Pull Requests).

mainEntity.name: How to become a Maintainer in openEuler?

acceptedAnswer.text: Becoming a Maintainer requires sustained high-quality contributions over time, recommendation by existing Maintainers, and approval through SIG group discussion. Maintainers handle code review, release management, and SIG direction decisions.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between Contributor and Committer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contributors submit Pull Requests to participate in code contributions but do not have merge permissions; Committers, after sustained contributions, gain merge permissions for specific modules and can review and merge other Contributors' PRs.
